title: Datacentre to Azure Migration Executive Summary: Lower Cost, Higher Agility
left_content: Move core banking workloads from legacy datacentres to Azure in phased waves to reduce infrastructure run-costs, improve delivery speed, and create a more elastic platform for future digital growth. The program standardizes landing zones, network segmentation, identity, and governance so teams can migrate safely while maintaining operational control.

right_cards.label: Potential infrastructure run-cost reduction after steady state optimization
Faster provisioning for new environments and workloads
Wave-based migration lowers execution risk and preserves business continuity

title: Resilience: Stronger Recovery, Better Availability, Less Single-Point Risk
left_content: Azure enables a more resilient operating model by distributing workloads across availability zones and regions, replacing dependency on a single datacentre with tested failover patterns. Recovery objectives improve through automated backup, orchestration, and repeatable disaster recovery runbooks aligned to critical banking services.

right_cards.label: Target availability for key platform services with zone-aware design
Recovery time improvement through automated failover and scripted recovery
Design pattern for business-critical services and disaster recovery

title: Security: Centralized Controls, Stronger Identity, and Better Auditability
left_content: The migration strengthens security by moving to centralized identity, least-privilege access, encryption by default, and continuous monitoring across the cloud estate. Azure policy, logging, and threat detection improve audit readiness while helping standardize controls across applications, networks, and data.

right_cards.label: Security model built around identity, device, workload, and network controls
Continuous monitoring and alerting for threats, misconfigurations, and anomalies
Governance and audit evidence across migrated environments
